description: Machine-readable PropWeaver public API contract. Use this file to verify method names, parameter types, and return types before calling PropWeaver from another project.

PropertyGraph.description:
SQLite-backed property graph database

Example:
    # Create graph and add nodes with properties
    with PropertyGraph() as graph:
        person = graph.add_node("person", name="Alice", age=30)
        city = graph.add_node("city", name="San Francisco")

        # Connect them with a relationship
        lives_in = graph.add_edge(person, "lives_in", city, since=2020)

        # Set graph-level metadata
        graph.props["project_name"] = "Social Network"
        graph.props["version"] = "1.0"

        # Query the graph
        results = list(graph.nodes("person", name="Alice"))
        schema_version = graph.props["schema_version"]  # Always available
    # Automatically closed when exiting with block

constructor.description:
Initialize PropertyGraph

Args:
    db_path: Path to database file. Special values:
        - None: in-memory database (default)
        - "": temporary file (auto-deleted)
        - "/path/to/file.db": persistent database file
    allowed_base_dir: Optional directory to restrict database files to.
        If specified, db_path must be within this directory.
        Use for additional security when paths come from untrusted input.

Raises:
    ValueError: If db_path contains path traversal or is outside allowed_base_dir

Security Note:
    Database paths are validated to prevent directory traversal attacks.
    Paths containing ".." are rejected. For additional security with
    untrusted input, specify allowed_base_dir to restrict file locations.

Example:
    # In-memory database (no file)
    graph = PropertyGraph()

    # Persistent database
    graph = PropertyGraph("my_graph.db")

    # Restricted to specific directory (secure mode)
    graph = PropertyGraph("user_123.db", allowed_base_dir="/var/lib/myapp")

add_edge.description:
Add an edge between two nodes with properties

Example:
    friendship = graph.add_edge(user1, "friends", user2, since="2023-01-01")

add_node.description:
Add a node to the graph with properties

Example:
    user = graph.add_node("user", name="Bob", email="bob@example.com")

edge_count.description:
Get total number of edges in the graph

Returns:
    Number of edges as int

edge_types.description:
Get list of all distinct edge types in the graph

Returns:
    List of edge type strings, sorted alphabetically

edges.description: Start a lazy iterator for edges

iter_edges.description:
Iterator over edges with optional filtering.

This is a convenience method that wraps the main `edges()` query builder.

Args:
    edge_type: Filter by edge type
    limit: Maximum number of edges to return
    **properties: Property filters

Example:
    for friendship in graph.iter_edges("friends", active=True):
        print(f"{friendship.src_id} -> {friendship.dst_id}")

node_count.description:
Get total number of nodes in the graph

Returns:
    Number of nodes as int

node_types.description:
Get list of all distinct node types in the graph

Returns:
    List of node type strings, sorted alphabetically

nodes.description: Start a lazy iterator for nodes (XPath-style)

resource_stats.description:
Get resource usage statistics for the graph

Returns dictionary with database size, entity counts, and property counts.
Useful for monitoring resource consumption and enforcing limits.

Returns:
    Dictionary with keys:
    - db_size_bytes: Database file size in bytes (0 for in-memory)
    - db_size_mb: Database file size in MB (0 for in-memory)
    - node_count: Total number of nodes
    - edge_count: Total number of edges
    - node_property_count: Total properties across all nodes
    - edge_property_count: Total properties across all edges
    - graph_property_count: Graph-level property count
    - total_entities: Sum of nodes and edges
    - total_properties: Sum of all properties

Example:
    stats = graph.resource_stats()
    print(f"Database size: {stats['db_size_mb']:.2f} MB")
    print(f"Total entities: {stats['total_entities']}")

    # Enforce limits
    if stats['node_count'] > MAX_NODES:
        raise ResourceLimitError("Too many nodes")

set_log_level.description:
Set logging level for all PropWeaver operations

Args:
    level: logging.DEBUG, logging.INFO, logging.SUMMARY (25), etc.

Example:
    import logging
    from propweaver.logging_utils import SUMMARY

    graph.set_log_level(logging.DEBUG)  # Show all SQL queries
    graph.set_log_level(SUMMARY)        # Token-efficient summaries only

timestamp.description:
Get graph creation timestamp

Returns:
    Unix timestamp as float

to_json.description:
Return graph summary as JSON-serializable dictionary

Shows first 'limit' nodes and edges with all their properties, plus graph metadata.

Args:
    limit: Maximum number of nodes and edges to include (default: 10)

Example:
    graph_data = graph.to_json(limit=5)
    print(json.dumps(graph_data, indent=2))

NodeProxy.note: Returned by PropertyGraph.add_node() and node iterators. Not instantiated directly.
NodeProxy.description: Lightweight proxy for a node in the graph

constructor.description: Initialize self.  See help(type(self)) for accurate signature.

timestamp.description:
Get node creation timestamp

Returns:
    Unix timestamp as float

to_json.description:
Return node data as JSON-serializable dictionary

Returns:
    Dictionary with node_id, node_type, and all properties

EdgeProxy.note: Returned by PropertyGraph.add_edge() and edge iterators. Not instantiated directly.
EdgeProxy.description: Lightweight proxy for an edge in the graph

constructor.description: Initialize self.  See help(type(self)) for accurate signature.

timestamp.description:
Get edge creation timestamp

Returns:
    Unix timestamp as float

to_json.description:
Return edge data as JSON-serializable dictionary

Returns:
    Dictionary with edge_id, edge_type, src_id, dst_id, and all properties

NodeIterator.note: Returned by PropertyGraph.nodes(). Supports method chaining and iteration.
NodeIterator.description: Lazy iterator for XPath-style graph traversal

constructor.description: Initialize self.  See help(type(self)) for accurate signature.

delete.description: Add DELETE step to query - returns new iterator

execute.description: Execute modification operations and return count of affected items

filter.description: Filter current result set - returns new iterator

incoming.description: Follow incoming edges - returns new iterator

outgoing.description: Follow outgoing edges - returns new iterator

EdgeIterator.note: Returned by PropertyGraph.edges(). Supports method chaining and iteration.

constructor.description: Initialize self.  See help(type(self)) for accurate signature.

delete.description: Add DELETE step to query - returns new iterator

execute.description: Execute modification operations and return count of affected edges

filter.description: Filter current result set - returns new iterator

query.description: Declarative query representation. Advanced use only — most callers use the fluent API on NodeIterator/EdgeIterator instead.

property_types.description: Python types that may be stored as node/edge/graph property values.

DatabaseError.description:
Low-level database operation failed.

Similar to SQLAlchemy's DBAPIError - wraps underlying database
errors while preserving the original exception and SQL context.

DoesNotExist.description:
Convenience exception similar to Django's DoesNotExist.

Can be used when the specific entity type doesn't matter,
or for generic "not found" scenarios.

EntityError.description:
Base class for entity-related errors (nodes and edges).

Provides common context for operations on graph entities.

EntityNotFoundError.description:
Entity (node or edge) does not exist in the database.

Similar to Django's DoesNotExist - indicates the requested entity
cannot be found. Includes entity type and ID for debugging.

IntegrityError.description:
Database constraint violation.

Similar to SQLAlchemy's IntegrityError - indicates a constraint
violation like foreign key, unique constraint, or check constraint.

InvalidQueryError.description:
Query is malformed or uses invalid operations.

Indicates a programming error in query construction.

PropWeaverError.description:
Base exception for all PropWeaver operations.

Similar to SQLAlchemy's SQLAlchemyError - provides the root of the exception
hierarchy and basic context storage.

PropertyError.description:
Base class for property-related operations.

Provides context about which property caused the error.

PropertyNotFoundError.description:
Property does not exist on the specified entity.

Includes available properties to help with debugging typos and
understanding the entity's structure.

Inherits from KeyError for backward compatibility.

PropertyValueError.description:
Property value is invalid or cannot be stored.

Includes information about the attempted value and why it's invalid.

Inherits from ValueError for backward compatibility.

QueryError.description:
Base class for query construction and execution errors.

Provides context about the query that failed.

QueryExecutionError.description:
Query failed during execution.

Similar to SQLAlchemy's StatementError but for graph queries.
Includes the original exception that caused the failure.

StatementError.description:
Error executing a graph operation or query.

Similar to SQLAlchemy's StatementError - provides context about the
operation that failed, including operation type and parameters.

TransactionError.description:
Transaction management error.

Indicates problems with transaction state, rollback, or commit operations.

ValidationError.description:
Data validation failed.

Indicates that data doesn't meet expected constraints or formats.
Can include multiple validation failures.

api_models.description: Pydantic v2 models for validating PropWeaver return values. Requires: pip install 'propweaver[api]'

EdgeModel.description:
Validated representation of a PropWeaver edge.

Mirrors the data returned by :class:`~propweaver.EdgeProxy`.

Validated representation of a PropWeaver edge.

Mirrors the data returned by :class:`~propweaver.EdgeProxy`.

edge_id.title: Edge Id

All counts reflect the state at the time of the call.

edge_property_count.description: Total properties stored across all edges

total_properties.title: Total Properties

GraphSummaryModel.description:
Full graph summary returned by ``PropertyGraph.to_json()``.

Contains graph metadata, a sample of nodes and edges, and totals.

PropWeaverAPIVersion.description:
Version metadata for the PropWeaver public API.

Embed this in downstream projects to record which API version was
targeted when the integration was written, enabling change detection.

json_schema.description:
Version metadata for the PropWeaver public API.

Embed this in downstream projects to record which API version was
targeted when the integration was written, enabling change detection.

api_schema_version.description: Version of this api.py schema module, incremented on breaking changes
api_schema_version.title: Api Schema Version

json_schema.title: PropWeaverAPIVersion

PropertyGraphConfig.description:
Parameters accepted by the :class:`~propweaver.PropertyGraph` constructor.

Use to validate configuration before opening a graph, or to document
graph instantiation in consuming projects.

json_schema.description:
Parameters accepted by the :class:`~propweaver.PropertyGraph` constructor.

Use to validate configuration before opening a graph, or to document
graph instantiation in consuming projects.

db_path.description: Path to the SQLite database file. None = in-memory (default), '' = auto-deleted temp file, '/path/to/file.db' = persistent file
db_path.title: Db Path

allowed_base_dir.description: Optional directory restriction for db_path. When set, db_path must resolve inside this directory. Raises ValueError for path traversal or out-of-bounds paths.
allowed_base_dir.title: Allowed Base Dir

json_schema.title: PropertyGraphConfig

QuerySpecModel.description:
Validated representation of a :class:`~propweaver.QuerySpec`.

Mirrors ``QuerySpec`` (a ``dataclass``) with full Pydantic validation.

QueryStepModel.description:
Validated representation of a single :class:`~propweaver.QueryStep`.

Mirrors ``QueryStep`` (a ``dataclass``) with full Pydantic validation.

edge_type.description: For FILTER/TRAVERSE steps: restrict to this edge type
edge_type.title: Edge Type

properties.description: For FILTER steps: property equality filters
properties.title: Properties

field.description: For ORDER steps: field name to sort by
field.title: Field
